channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Woolen's late interception seals win as Seahawks beat struggling Bears 6-3 to boost playoff hopes
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Riq Woolen intercepted Caleb Williams' late pass and the Seattle Seahawks boosted their fading playoff hopes, squeezing past Chicago 6-3 on Thursday night to hand the Bears their 10th straight loss.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pat Riley says the Miami Heat will not trade Jimmy Butler
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — If Jimmy Butler wants a trade, the Miami Heat have no plans to make him happy. Heat President Pat Riley — in a rare move — spoke out to address rumors Thursday, saying the team has no plans to trade Butler.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pro Picks: Chargers over Patriots is the best bet and Broncos and Vikings will pull off upsets
item.description: Pro Picks is a weekly column where AP Pro Football Writer Rob Maaddi shares his picks for upcoming games. For all previous Pro Picks, head here. ___ Playoff berths, draft positioning and more are up for grabs in Week 17.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Organizers say two sailors have died in Sydney to Hobart yacht race amid wild weather conditions
item.description: SYDNEY (AP) — Organizers said Friday that two Sydney to Hobart sailors have died at sea amid wild weather conditions that forced line honors favorite Master Lock Comanche to withdraw among mass retirements.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL Hall of Famer calls out George Pickens amid Steelers three-game slide
item.description: NFL legend Terrell Owens called out Steelers wide receiver George Pickens for his mishaps in Pittsburgh's third straight loss, this time against the Kansas City Chiefs Christmas Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Durant and Beal score 27 points each, Suns beat Nuggets 110-100 to close out Christmas slate
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Kevin Durant and Bradley Beal each scored 27 points and the Phoenix Suns beat the Denver Nuggets 110-100 on Wednesday night in the nightcap to the NBA’s Christmas slate of games.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Christmas Day classic shows why ‘LeBron and Steph’ still the best matchup in NBA
item.description: Lakers forward LeBron James on Stephen Curry’s late-game shotmaking: “You just tip your hat, because he’s a great player and you just have to tip your hat”
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: Beyoncé's finger-gun gesture during NFL halftime show sparks jokes among fans
item.description: NFL fans all made the same jokes Wednesday as they watched Beyoncé make a finger-gun gesture at the end of her halftime show performance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reports: Pete Carroll interested in Bears' head-coaching job
item.description: Former Seattle Seahawks head coach Pete Carroll has expressed interest in becoming the next head coach of the Chicago Bears, who are looking to fill their head coaching vacancy for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: BYU arrives in San Antonio ahead of Alamo Bowl
item.description: SAN ANTONIO, Tex. (ABC4 Sports) - The Cougars have arrived in San Antonio for what they hope will be an Alamo Bowl to remember. "Super excited to be down here," said defensive end Tyler Batty.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Breaking: Dave Fleming leaves CMU after 20 years
item.description: "I thank Coach Fleming for all his dedication and hard work at CMU," Colorado Mesa Athletic Director Joan McDermott said in a press release. "He is not only a great coach, but a special person and will be greatly missed.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Kiser, KREX 5

item.title: Jazz winning streak ends at Cleveland, 124-113
item.description: CLEVELAND (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz first winning streak of the season did not last very long. Darius Garland scored 23 points and the NBA-best Cleveland Cavaliers beat the Utah Jazz 124-113 on Monday night for their 17th victory in 18 home games.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bucs with Bowles: Mistakes cost Bucs a game and their division lead
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers four-game winning streak came to an end Sunday night with a 26-24 loss to the Dallas Cowboys.
item.dc:creator: Dan Lucas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utah blows 3-goal lead in shootout loss to Anaheim
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Robby Fabbri scored twice, and the Anaheim Ducks rallied from down three goals to defeat the Utah Hockey Club 5-4 in a shootout at Delta Center on Sunday. “I think we just didn't stray away from our game,” said Fabbri.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Tatum scores a season-high 43 to go with 16 rebounds, 10 assist in Celtics 123-98 win over Bulls
item.description: Jayson Tatum scored a season-high 43 points, grabbed 16 rebounds and dished out 10 assists for his third career triple-double and the Boston Celtics beat the Chicago Bulls 123-98 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz win back-to-back games for first time this season
item.description: BROOKLYN, N.Y. (ABC4 Sports) - For the first time since April, the Utah Jazz are on a winning streak. Lauri Markkanen scored 21 points, Collin Sexton and Svi Mykhailiuk each had 18 points, and the Utah Jazz beat the Brooklyn Nets 105-94 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Mahomes throws for TD and runs for score as Chiefs beat Texans 27-19 to close in on No. 1 seed
item.description: Patrick Mahomes knew he could run on the ankle he sprained last week. The bigger concern for the Chiefs quarterback was how he would stop, when all the weight and pressure and momentum would land squarely upon it.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: First baseman Paul Goldschmidt and the Yankees agree to a $12.5 million, 1-year deal, AP source says
item.description: First baseman Paul Goldschmidt and the New York Yankees agreed Saturday to a $12.5 million, one-year contract, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oakland legend, Hall of Famer Rickey Henderson has died at the age of 65
item.description: (KRON) – Rickey Henderson, one of the greatest baseball players to ever live, died at age 65. Henderson is Major League Baseball’s all-time stolen base leader and is considered by many to be the greatest player in A’s franchise history.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Bronny James is quietly having a solid December in the G League
item.description: Even underneath the shade of his legendary father, the spotlight on LeBron "Bronny" James Jr. was perhaps too much for any young player at the start of his professional basketball career — but months later, it seems as though he's figuring things out his own way.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Pro pickleball players now earn more than WNBA, NWSL players
item.description: (KRON) -- The pickleball craze that's swept across the Bay Area and nation in recent years has apparently paid off, especially for female athletes. According to the Professional Pickleball Association, pro female players on the PPA Tour earned an average of $260,000 in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Bulls beat Celtics 117-108 in Boston
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Celtics forward Jaylen Brown took issue with back-to-back technical fouls called on coach Joe Mazzulla and himself late in the fourth quarter of Boston’s 117-108 loss to the Chicago Bulls on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mikheyev stars as 'Hawks beat Kraken 3-1 for 3rd straight win
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Ilya Mikheyev scored two more goals, Arvid Soderblom made 27 saves and the Chicago Blackhawks beat the Seattle Kraken 3-1 on Thursday night for their third straight win. Teuvo Teravainen had two assists as Chicago closed out a perfect three-game homestand.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
